source_paper_title: A Unified Analysis of a Class of Proximal Bundle Methods for Solving Hybrid Convex Composite Optimization Problems
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Liang_2024, title={A Unified Analysis of a Class of Proximal Bundle Methods for Solving Hybrid Convex Composite Optimization Problems}, volume={49}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2023.1372}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2023.1372}, number={2}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Liang, Jiaming and Monteiro, Renato D. context:
Section 6 (Concluding Remarks), page 21 (paragraph beginning 'First, under the assumption that the diameter D of dom h is finite, ...').

It would be interesting to design an accelerated variant of GPB which is optimal for the aforementioned HCCO problem class.
title: Design accelerated proximal bundle methods for hybrid convex composite optimization problems
background_latex:
Let \(f,h:\mathbb{R}^n\to\mathbb{R}\cup\{+\infty\}\) be proper lower semicontinuous convex functions with \(\mathrm{dom}\,h\subseteq \mathrm{dom}\,f\). Consider the hybrid convex composite optimization problem
\[
\phi^*:=\min\{\phi(x):=f(x)+h(x): x\in\mathbb{R}^n\}.
\]
Assume \(h\) is \(\mu\)-convex for some \(\mu\ge 0\), meaning that \(h(x)-\frac{\mu}{2}\|x\|^2\) is convex. Assume a first-order oracle \(f'\) is available with \(f'(x)\in \partial f(x)\) for all \(x\in\mathrm{dom}\,h\), and there exist parameters \(M_f\ge 0\) and \(L_f\ge 0\) such that for all \(u,v\in\mathrm{dom}\,h\),
\[
\|f'(u)-f'(v)\|\le 2M_f + L_f\|u-v\|.
\]
Let \(D\) denote the diameter of \(\mathrm{dom}\,h\) when it is finite:
\[
D:=\sup\{\|x-y\|: x,y\in \mathrm{dom}\,h\}<\infty.
\]
An \(\bar\varepsilon\)-solution is a point \(\bar x\in\mathrm{dom}\,h\) with \(\phi(\bar x)-\phi^*\le \bar\varepsilon\).

A proximal bundle (PB) method is a first-order method that iteratively solves proximalized subproblems based on a piecewise-linear (bundle) lower model of \(f\) plus \(h\), typically producing serious/null steps; the paper analyzes a generic PB framework (GPB) and obtains iteration-complexity bounds of order
\[
O\!\left(\min\left\{\frac{(M_f^2+\bar\varepsilon L_f)d_0^2}{\bar\varepsilon^2},\;\left(\frac{M_f^2+\bar\varepsilon L_f}{\mu\bar\varepsilon}+1\right)\log\left(\frac{\mu d_0^2}{\bar\varepsilon}+1\right)\right\}+1\right)
\]
(up to logarithmic factors), where \(d_0\) is the distance from the initial point to the solution set.

For the same HCCO class with finite \(D\), known lower bounds (cited in the paper) imply that an optimal (accelerated) rate in terms of \((L_f,M_f,D,\bar\varepsilon)\) is
\[
O\!\left( \frac{\sqrt{L_f}\,D}{\sqrt{\bar\varepsilon}} + \frac{M_f^2 D^2}{\bar\varepsilon^2}\right)
\]
(in the sense described in the cited reference).
problem_definition_latex:
Assume \(D:=\sup\{\|x-y\|:x,y\in\mathrm{dom}\,h\}<\infty\). Develop a (proximal) bundle-type method for minimizing \(\phi(x)=f(x)+h(x)\) under the hybrid subgradient condition \(\|f'(u)-f'(v)\|\le 2M_f+L_f\|u-v\|\) whose iteration complexity to obtain an \(\bar\varepsilon\)-solution matches (up to universal constants and allowable logarithmic factors) the optimal bound
\[
O\!\left( \frac{\sqrt{L_f}\,D}{\sqrt{\bar\varepsilon}} + \frac{M_f^2 D^2}{\bar\varepsilon^2}\right).
\]
Equivalently, design an accelerated variant of the generic proximal bundle framework achieving the above complexity for the HCCO problem class parameterized by \((L_f,M_f,D)\).

location_in_paper: Section 6 (Concluding Remarks), page 21 (paragraph beginning 'First, under the assumption that the diameter D of dom h is finite, ...').
open_problem_quote: It would be interesting to design an accelerated variant of GPB which is optimal for the aforementioned HCCO problem class.
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The question asks for designing an algorithm with a provable worst-case complexity guarantee, not for refuting a conjectured inequality. Automated instance search is unlikely to yield decisive counterexamples because the claim concerns universal worst-case rates over a broad function class.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: There is a searchable design space (bundle update rules, momentum/estimate-sequence parameters, adaptive prox stepsizes) and a computable proxy fitness signal via empirical performance on representative synthetic HCCO instances. However, the target is a worst-case theoretical rate, and empirical optimization may not translate into provable acceleration without substantial analytic insight.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
The open problem asks for an accelerated proximal bundle-type method for the hybrid convex composite optimization (HCCO) model \(\min_x \phi(x)=f(x)+h(x)\) under the hybrid subgradient condition \(\|f'(u)-f'(v)\|\le 2M_f+L_f\|u-v\|\), assuming \(\mathrm{dom}\,h\) has finite diameter \(D\). Liang–Monteiro (2023) provide a unified iteration-complexity analysis for a generic proximal bundle (GPB) framework (including one-cut, two-cut, and multi-cut updates) with complexity essentially \(O((M_f^2+\bar\varepsilon L_f)\,d_0^2/\bar\varepsilon^2)\) (and a strong-convexity refinement when \(\mu>0\)), which is optimal in the purely nonsmooth case \(L_f=0\) but not optimal when \(L_f>0\) and \(D<\infty\).

The benchmark optimal rate on bounded domains is given by Lan’s accelerated composite subgradient methodology, yielding \(O(\sqrt{L_f}D/\sqrt{\bar\varepsilon}+M_f^2D^2/\bar\varepsilon^2)\) and accompanied by matching lower bounds for the \((L_f,M_f,D)\)-parameterized class. Separately, the bundle-level line of work (Lemar{\'e}chal–Nemirovski–Nesterov 1995; Lan 2015) shows that cutting-plane/bundle mechanisms can be made uniformly optimal over compact sets, achieving accelerated dependence on \(L\) without proximal regularization. On the proximal bundle side, optimal nonsmooth rates (including strong convexity refinements) have been established for several PB variants (e.g., Liang–Monteiro 2021; D{\'i}az–Grimmer 2021), but these results do not incorporate the accelerated \(\sqrt{L_f}D/\sqrt{\bar\varepsilon}\) term in the hybrid regime.

Overall, with the information available here (and no forward-citation evidence of a later solution), the problem remains open: there is not yet a proximal bundle method for HCCO on bounded domains whose proven iteration complexity matches \(O(\sqrt{L_f}D/\sqrt{\bar\varepsilon}+M_f^2D^2/\bar\varepsilon^2)\) up to constants/log factors. A promising direction is to hybridize GPB’s serious/null-step and proximal stabilization machinery with bundle-level gap-reduction/level-set ideas that are known to yield acceleration on compact domains, while preserving the hybrid-model structure \(f+h\) and the oracle model implicit in \(\|f'(u)-f'(v)\|\le 2M_f+L_f\|u-v\|\).

sources.title: An optimal method for stochastic composite optimization
sources.theorem_or_result: Proposes an accelerated composite subgradient framework achieving (in the deterministic bounded-domain specialization) complexity \(O(\sqrt{L_f}D/\sqrt{\bar\varepsilon}+M_f^2D^2/\bar\varepsilon^2)\) for hybrid smooth+nonsmooth convex composite minimization over a compact domain, and argues optimality (via lower bounds) for the \((L_f,M_f,D)\)-parameterized class.
sources.connection: Provides the target optimal rate and the lower-bound benchmark cited by Liang–Monteiro; serves as the complexity yardstick that an accelerated proximal bundle method for HCCO with finite diameter \(D\) should match.

sources.title: Bundle-level type methods uniformly optimal for smooth and nonsmooth convex optimization
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops bundle-level methods that are uniformly optimal across smooth and nonsmooth convex regimes over compact domains, attaining accelerated \(O(\sqrt{L}D/\sqrt{\varepsilon})\) behavior in the smooth case while retaining nonsmooth optimality, using level-set/bundle models without proximal regularization.
sources.connection: Shows that bundle-type (cutting-plane) mechanisms can be made uniformly optimal on bounded domains; suggests design patterns (level control, gap reduction) that might be hybridized with proximal bundle (serious/null) schemes to reach the Lan (2012) optimal bound.

sources.title: New variants of bundle methods
sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces bundle-level method variants (including level and proximal stabilization ideas) and establishes complexity-type guarantees for convex nonsmooth minimization over compact sets, forming a foundation for later optimal bundle-level schemes.
sources.connection: Historical origin of bundle-level acceleration ideas; relevant because accelerated rates on bounded domains were first achieved in this line, and the open problem asks for an accelerated proximal bundle analogue for HCCO.

sources.title: A proximal bundle variant with optimal iteration-complexity for a large range of prox stepsizes
sources.theorem_or_result: Analyzes a resolvent proximal bundle (RPB) method (multiple-cuts) for nonsmooth composite convex optimization (effectively \(L_f=0\)) and proves optimal \(O(\bar\varepsilon^{-2})\) iteration complexity for a broad range of prox stepsizes, with refined dependence on strong convexity \(\mu\) up to logarithms.
sources.connection: Demonstrates that proximal bundle frameworks can be made iteration-optimal in the pure nonsmooth regime; serves as a stepping stone toward hybrid \((M_f,L_f)\) and accelerated \(\sqrt{L_f}/\sqrt{\bar\varepsilon}\) behavior sought in the open problem.

sources.title: Optimal convergence rates for the proximal bundle method
sources.theorem_or_result: Establishes optimal (up to constants) convergence rates for a classical proximal bundle method in nonsmooth convex optimization, sharpening earlier \(\tilde O(\bar\varepsilon^{-1})\) strong-convexity results and clarifying parameter choices for optimality.
sources.connection: Confirms that proximal bundle can be tuned to achieve optimal nonsmooth rates; highlights what is missing for the hybrid smooth+nonsmooth accelerated term \(O(\sqrt{L_f}D/\sqrt{\bar\varepsilon})\) in bounded-domain HCCO.

sources.title: Rate of convergence of the bundle method
sources.theorem_or_result: Provides convergence-rate analysis for (proximal) bundle methods, including strong convexity regimes and step-size choices, with bounds that were later tightened to optimality by subsequent work.
sources.connection: Part of the proximal bundle complexity lineage referenced by Liang–Monteiro; useful for understanding stabilization mechanisms but does not deliver the accelerated \(\sqrt{L_f}/\sqrt{\bar\varepsilon}\) term for hybrid HCCO.
